page: Microsoft Azure EC96ias_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance belonging to the ECIASv5 server family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor with an x86_64 architecture, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning it relies entirely on external storage options, though it is explicitly designated as Premium Storage capable. There are no integrated GPUs present on this instance, and it does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its isolated architecture and high memory capacity of 672.0 GB, the EC96ias_v5 is designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring dedicated compute resources. It is suitable for large-scale databases, enterprise applications, and memory-heavy analytical processing. The isolated nature of the hardware provides physical security boundaries, offering high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise workloads that require strict isolation without the overhead of multi-tenant interference.
description: Microsoft Azure EC96ias_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance from the ECIASv5 family, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by an AMD processor with an x86_64 architecture and running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 96 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of system memory, resulting in 7.0 GB of RAM per core. The server does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing high-performance external disk attachments. It contains no GPUs and offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its isolated hardware allocation, the EC96ias_v5 is optimized for secure, large-scale database deployments, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-heavy data processing tasks that demand dedicated compute resources and strict physical isolation.
